blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: Shirone?
Kanya's voice trembled.
It was definitely Shirone. But how could he be here?

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: No, that didn't matter anymore.
If the person on the screen was Shirone, it meant new hope for the despairing rebels.

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: Unit 5 is down! Provide cover!
Hearing the radio, Kanya snapped back to reality and turned her head to the multivision.
Most of the units were immobilized, but they still had functioning weapons.
And they were all aiming at Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: Wait, no!
Kanya hurriedly pressed the radio button.

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: Don't shoot! He's not an enemy!
When there was no response, she wasn't reassured even after sending the message.
She checked on Shirone.
He was standing there confidently, fully exposed.

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: Damn it...!
She pressed the emergency escape button, and the reinforced glass of the cockpit shot forward with a pop.
Detaching herself from the Piper in the Guroi's synchro machine, Kanya crawled out of the bushes, raising her hands.

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: Stop! Cease fire!
The voice of the squad leader came from the speaker of Unit 5.
Click! Click! Click!
Kanya closed her eyes tightly, but all she heard from the remaining three Gurois was the sound of metal clinking.

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: What... what is this?
Looking around in confusion, Kanya realized the scenery was distorting.
At the center of the distortion, a pair of giant gears were interlocked and spinning.
Sein's Equilibrium had neutralized the chemical reactions in the gas.

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: Duds! They're duds, sir!

blocks.speaker: Squad Leader
blocks.content: Same here. Check the ignition plugs.

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: No issues! Damn it...! Ugh!
Boom!
Kuan and Etella took down one of the Gurois.
Though it took longer than expected given their skills, it was significant that the two, specialized in fighting large creatures, had destroyed the machine with their physical abilities.
The reinforced glass of the Guroi popped out.
The pilot, hanging from the synchro machine, was unconscious, and Armin stood beside him.
With precise coordinates, a spacetime mage could infiltrate even a sealed space.
The other team members also easily destroyed the Gurois, leaving only the squad leader's unit intact.
Gaold approached the Guroi with interest, stroking his chin as if encountering something unknown.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Get down. If you don't want to die.
Pop!
The reinforced glass shot up to the ceiling as the squad leader, still attached to the Piper, lunged at Gaold.

blocks.speaker: Squad Leader
blocks.content: Die!

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: No, sir!
Kanya shouted, but the squad leader's sigma was already descending towards Gaold's head.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Kanya?
Gaold looked at Shirone. Then, as if it were a separate matter, his fist swung swiftly, striking the squad leader's jaw.

blocks.speaker: Squad Leader
blocks.content: Ugh!
He fell pathetically, rolling on the ground with unfocused eyes.
If Gaold hadn't deactivated his press magic, the squad leader's face would have been obliterated.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Do you know her, Shirone?
Shirone squinted, looking more closely.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Kanya? Is that you, Kanya?
Kanya, finally relaxing, walked weakly towards him.
Thunk! Thunk! She detached herself from the Piper and ran into Shirone's arms.

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: Shirone, Shirone! Waaah!
The sudden shift from a deadly confrontation to a sweet reunion left everyone bewildered.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: Is it really... Shirone?
There wasn't a single rebel who didn't know Shirone.
The first Nephilim to stop the Elixir of Myths.
The rebellion had been organized after the riots in Shamain District 73, and they called Shirone the 'Light of District 73,' honoring his legacy.

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: Sorry. I didn't recognize you with that strange outfit.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It's okay. Anyone would have reacted the same if they met an enemy in disguise.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It's okay. How's Lena?

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: She's with the main force. She'll be thrilled to see you.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Haha! And your parents?

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: Dad's still going strong.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: ...I see.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Even back then, she didn't have much time left, so he could guess what had happened since Kanya didn't mention it.
There is no good death in this world.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But Shirone hoped she had left happily, surrounded by her family.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: How long has it been since I left?

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: Exactly 432 days.

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: Kanya didn't need to think long. The day Shirone stopped the Elixir of Myths was monumental for the rebels, so there was no need to calculate.
'432 days.'
It wasn't much different from the time where Shirone had lived.
Goffin's Gate distorted space, so there was no time discrepancy due to distance.
The slightly faster time here was likely due to the gravitational field around the planet.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I heard you're at war with Heaven, but I haven't seen any angels or Maras on the way here. Why is that?

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: We don't know either. We fought fiercely, or rather, we mostly ran, but one day the angels just stopped appearing. Even on the full moon when they always patrolled.

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: When exactly did this change start?

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: About 130 days ago?
130 days.
Comparing it to the original world's timeline, it was roughly three months ago.
That was also when they lost contact with Gaold's loyalists who had infiltrated Heaven.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: 'Something definitely happened in Heaven. What is it? Is it related to Miro? Or...'

blocks.speaker: Squad Leader
blocks.content: This is bad. The damage to the unit is severe.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I'm sorry.

blocks.speaker: Squad Leader
blocks.content: It's not your fault. We struck first.

blocks.speaker: Squad Leader
blocks.content: But that man created a bizarre illusion first. If you experienced it, you'd see why anyone would think he was an enemy.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: But you aimed the arc first.

blocks.speaker: Squad Leader
blocks.content: He provoked us first.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: You misunderstood first.

blocks.speaker: Squad Leader
blocks.content: How could we not misunderstand with a face like that! He approached like he was going to hit us!
A moment of silence followed.

blocks.speaker: Squad Leader
blocks.content: Enough. Let's recover the units and rejoin the main force. They're not in a good situation either.

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: Where is the main force?

blocks.speaker: Squad Leader
blocks.content: The Plains of the Dead. They're currently conducting a giant extermination operation.

blocks.speaker: Squad Leader
blocks.content: This is the only unit ready for immediate deployment. I'll lead the way... huh?
Gaold was standing behind the squad leader.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Move. I'm taking it.

blocks.speaker: Squad Leader
blocks.content: What? You're going to pilot this?

blocks.speaker: Kangnam
blocks.content: Why do you want to pilot that? It's synced with the Piper, so you can't move it even if you get in.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Then give me the Piper too.

blocks.speaker: Kangnam
blocks.content: Let him do as he wants.
ΓÇö Five minutes later ΓÇö
Clang! Clang!
Gaold, equipped with the Piper, boarded the Guroi.
The squad leader, having lost everything, could only stare into the distance.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Is this really okay? We've just calmed things down, but taking their equipment seems...

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: He must have a reason. He wouldn't mess things up just out of curiosity, right?
Plu, feeling a pang of conscience, eventually changed her tune.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: Actually, I don't know either. But everyone here followed him because he's that kind of leader. I just trust him too.
Gaold connected the Piper to the synchro machine.
As he moved his arms inward, the Guroi mimicked the motion.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Not bad.
Without the reinforced glass, Gaold observed the surroundings as he moved the vehicle.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Hmm, how about this?
He rotated the waist joint to its limit, making the Guroi spin like a top.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Hehe. Kangnam, look at this.
When Gaold playfully attacked with the Guroi's arm, Kangnam dodged with a slight lean and couldn't help but shout.

blocks.speaker: Kangnam
blocks.content: Ugh, seriously! Should I just destroy it?

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: Please don't. The Gurois are precious equipment.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Fine. This guy is stressing me out.
Gaold finally examined various aspects of the Guroi.
While not comparable to the synesthesia of the spirit zone, the multivision provided excellent visibility, and the firepower was comparable to an air gun.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: How many of these do the rebels have?

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: That's classified. I can't tell you.
It was impossible to tell where the joke ended and the seriousness began.
With a clanking sound, Guroi's gun barrel pointed at the platoon leader.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Even if it's confidential, I don't know. At best, I know the battalion's equipment status. How could a mere platoon leader know the total production of the rebel army?
Gaold smacked his lips and stopped the machine.
Jumping down from Guroi, he threw off his piper and quietly joined Shirone's group.

blocks.speaker: Kangnam
blocks.content: Why? You acted like you were going to ride all day.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: I'm getting motion sickness. It's worse than talking.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: This was the power of the Guroi unit that Gaold calculated was needed to capture a single Heavenly Angel.
At dusk, Shirone's group emerged from the forest.

When they reached a hill overlooking the Plain of the Dead, they saw the scene of the giants' procession and the 23rd Battalion of the 1st Command of the rebel army engaging in localized battles stretching to the horizon.
The battalion had a total of thirty Gurois.
However, since one team, including Kanya, was sent as support troops, only twenty-five Gurois were currently in operation.
The Guroi unit, divided into five teams, cooperated with the infantry to lure a few giants at least one kilometer away from the procession and then concentrated their fire.
Every time the giants fell, torn to shreds, Shirone made a horrified expression.

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: Giants were also human.
The Mecha tribe members, who were once citizens, knew this better than anyone, so Kanya's face was also shadowed.

blocks.speaker: Platoon Leader
blocks.content: The rule is to eliminate giants immediately upon discovery. If they reach Jotunheim and achieve mental unity, they will wield the power of giants.
The platoon leader spoke as if making an excuse, but he wasn't wrong.
If eight egos overlapped, the schema's diagram would become as powerful as an excellent swordsman.
At that point, a mere battalion's force wouldn't be able to handle it.
The group quickly returned to the main unit after entering the plain.
Seeing the group return with only one Guroi, the company commander initially looked incredulous, then eventually exploded in anger.

blocks.speaker: Company Commander
blocks.content: What happened! You said it was a Kergoin! I sent five units to deal with it quickly, not to handle it like this!

blocks.speaker: Platoon Leader
blocks.content: I'm sorry.

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: Company commander! I have something to report!

blocks.speaker: Company Commander
blocks.content: Who are you! Can't you see I'm scolding the platoon leader! Are you ignoring the command structure!

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: Shirone has returned!

blocks.speaker: Company Commander
blocks.content: What? What nonsense are you spouting now?

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: Shirone has returned!
